subparts.text: Who are the three actors of industrial relations? Explain their roles in the industrial relations system.
Explain the causes of declining role of trade unions after globalization.
Explain the machinery available for the settlement of industrial conflict.
Define the term “collective bargaining.” What are the characteristics of collective bargaining? What are the principles of collective bargaining.
Write a short note on any two Wage theories with suitable current implications in a business environment.
Explain the differences between the Employee Provident Fund, Employee Pension Scheme, and Employee Deposit Linked Insurance Scheme under the EPF Act.

questions.question_text: Compare and contrast conciliation, arbitration, and adjudication as mechanisms for dispute resolution under the Industrial Disputes Act.
Provide the positive side of the trade unions in ensuring harmonious industrial relations.

subparts.text: What aspects of fairness or justice must the management team keep in mind while proposing and persuading the union to accept the various options?
What strategies can the management team use to persuade the union to sign this settlement?

questions.question_text: What is the role of the Works Committee and Joint Management Councils as per the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947? How does they help in resolving disputes at the workplace level?
Define the term “wages” under the Payment of Wages Act, 1936, and explain the responsibilities of the employer with regard to the mode and manner of wage payment to the worker.
Summarize the provisions of the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, relating to entitlement for gratuity, its rates and the condition under which it can be forfeited.
Provide the provisions under Factories Act, 1948 related to health, sanitation, and safety of workers.
